title: Sleep Quality and Depressive Symptoms in College Students
abstract: This article examines the relationship between sleep quality and depressive symptoms among college students. The study discusses how poor sleep habits may be associated with higher self-reported depression and reduced academic functioning.

title: Anxiety Symptoms and Academic Stress in University Populations
abstract: This study reviews anxiety symptoms among university students and considers the role of academic stress, social pressure, and coping behaviors. Findings suggest that stress management and support services may reduce anxiety-related outcomes.

title: Mental Health and Well-Being During the COVID-19 Pandemic
abstract: This article focuses on changes in mental health and well-being during the COVID-19 pandemic. The paper highlights increased reports of anxiety, depression, isolation, and uncertainty across young adult populations.

title: Digital Tools for Mental Health Screening and Support
abstract: This paper discusses the growing use of digital tools in mental health screening and support. The article reviews benefits such as accessibility and limitations such as privacy, accuracy, and ethical concerns.

title: Depression, Sleep Disturbance, and Student Well-Being
abstract: This study explores links between sleep disturbance, depressive symptoms, and overall student well-being. The findings emphasize the importance of sleep hygiene and early identification of mental health concerns.

title: Social Media Use and Anxiety Among Young Adults
abstract: This article investigates associations between social media use and anxiety among young adults. The paper discusses possible relationships between screen time, comparison, social isolation, and emotional distress.

title: AI-Assisted Literature Review Methods in Healthcare Research
abstract: This paper reviews the use of artificial intelligence tools to support literature review tasks in healthcare research. The study discusses text summarization, information extraction, article screening, and challenges related to accuracy.

title: Large Language Models for Scientific Text Summarization
abstract: This article examines the use of large language models for summarizing scientific text. The paper highlights potential benefits in efficiency but also notes concerns related to hallucinations, missing context, and verification.
